_comment: Path patterns where a published source-language package may legitimately ship native binaries. Anywhere else (and especially repo root / tools / scripts / bin) is anomalous for source distributions and a strong signal under composite scoring when paired with an install hook that executes the binary. Operators can extend freely. Format: list of {pattern: glob, reason: text, severity_suppress: bool}.
_format: Each entry's `pattern` is a recursive glob relative to the manifest's parent directory; `**/<pattern>` semantics apply at any depth. `magic_required` (optional) restricts the allowlist to files whose first bytes match the named format — e.g. a `.wasm` slot only suppresses true WebAssembly, not an ELF wearing a wasm extension.

patterns.reason: WebAssembly module; legitimate in JS and Rust packages
node-gyp build output (committed prebuild); standard layout `build/Release/<name>.node`
alternative node-pre-gyp install dir (some packages drop the `lib/` prefix)
vendored dependency tree (Go convention, also common in Cargo and JS)
installed JS deps; usually excluded from publish but check independently if present in a published tarball
nested test fixture (any ``fixtures/`` dir at any depth)
git internal — typically excluded from publish but harmless if shipped

manifest_opt_in_fields._comment: Manifest fields that, when present, declare the package opts in to shipping platform-specific binaries. A package with one of these explicit declarations is treated as having a documented native-binary surface; we don't flag binaries it ships under expected paths.

name_suffix_opt_in._comment: Per-platform packages whose name explicitly encodes the target OS/arch — esbuild, swc, bun, etc. publish one tiny package per platform whose entire purpose is shipping the corresponding binary. We suppress for these by name pattern.
